TextENGNew: I woke up yet again.
No, perhaps it was better that I was awake.@k If I kept sleeping, the danger would only grow.
Tomorrow morning would be rough, but I couldn't trade my safety for comfort.
If someone were to break in...
Even if I were awake when an intruder came, I had no idea how much I could handle alone.
Still, it was better than lying there asleep and defenseless.@k At the very least, I wanted to give my nerves a moment's rest.
At this rate, the unbearable tension would drive me mad before morning came.
...Patter, patter, patter...
No, it had to be my imagination.
I was disgusted with myself, wondering how many times I'd thought that tonight.
...Patter, patter, patter, patter...
...The sound seemed to be drawing closer, little by little...
Was it just my imagination that this felt more real than the earlier hallucinations...?
Patter, patter, patter, patter.
The sound stopped right in front of my room with an abrupt 'click,' and I realized...
That was no hallucination!
Aah!!!!
``...Keiichi?@k@vS17/00/112700008. Wake up. It's morning.``
It was Mom.@k Morning had already come.
In the end, I had barely slept at all.@k Just thinking about battling drowsiness all day was enough to fill me with dread.
But at the same time, I felt a wave of relief at having made it safely through to morning.
I dragged myself downstairs.
The same ordinary scene as always spread out before me.
The same dining table, the same breakfast. Nothing had changed.
``You look so sleepy, Keiichi.@k@vS17/00/112700010. What time did you go to bed last night?``
``Y-yeah...@k@vS17/01/110100333. I just... had trouble falling asleep last night...``
``Oh? That's unusual for you, isn't it?``
``...Before I knew it, morning had come.@k@vS17/01/110100335. H-haaah...``
``Don't tell me you couldn't sleep because you were scared of the thunder?``
``No way!@k@vS17/01/110100337. I'm not a little kid...``
It felt like Mom had seen right through me, and I squirmed uncomfortably.
It wasn't the thunder that had kept me awake.
But the fact remained that it was fear that robbed me of sleep.
Sounds that couldn't possibly be there, or that were too trivial to matter.
The faintest rustle of treetops somewhere in the distance was enough to make me flinch.
...It had been a long, long night.
I tried to focus my attention on the morning sunlight and the chirping of birds.
And in doing so, I could see just how cowardly I'd been...
A tranquil, wholesome morning without a single incident.
Fear...
Fear of the Sonozaki family...
...Fear of Mion.
The dark side of the Sonozaki family that had been revealed to me.
The one who told me about it was none other than Mion Sonozaki herself.
In person.
A warning, or perhaps a threat...
It could be taken either way.
changeReason: Changes from 'I took it both ways' (active interpretation) to 'It could be taken either way' (passive observation); different agency.

TextENGNew: And yet...
Nothing had happened since then.
My everyday life continued as it always had.
Did I really have any reason to be afraid of Mion?
This morning was no different from any other, was it?
Perhaps I really was overthinking things...
``Keiichi-kun~, good morning~!``
``Hey. Morning, Rena.``
``Huh? Keiichi-kun, your eyes are all red.@k@vS17/02/110200147. What happened? What happened?``
``It's nothing... just couldn't fall asleep.@k@vS17/01/110100340. So I'm running on no sleep today. ...H-haaah~.``
``Are you okay? Are you?``
Rena leaned in close, peering at my face.
For a moment, my heart skipped a beat.
If someone got this close to Rena, she'd turn red in an instant. But apparently, when she was the one closing the distance, she didn't mind at all.
Before the color of my own face could change, I pulled away.
``Hey, Rena.@k@vS17/01/110100342. ...Mion's late, huh?``
``I wonder if she's still not feeling well? ...I wonder?``
I tilted my head vaguely in response.
According to Ooishi-san, Mion had disappeared. If that was true, there was no way she'd show up here.
``It's past the time we agreed to go on ahead without her.@k@vS17/02/110200151. ...What should we do?``
Rena asked with an innocent expression.
Did Rena really not know about Mion's disappearance...? Or was she deliberately pretending not to...?
As if to pull me from my thoughts, Rena turned to me and spoke.
changeReason: Changes Rena's action from 'encouraged me to think it over' to 'pulled me from my thoughts'; opposite direction of intent.

TextENGNew: ``What do you think, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS17/02/110200153. ...If we don't leave now, we'll be late.``
``Yeah, you're right.@k@vS17/01/110100344. ...Maybe Mion still isn't feeling well...``
...There's no point dwelling on things I can't do anything about.
I resolved that firmly.
...That's right. Wasn't I the one who wished so earnestly to simply enjoy these peaceful days?
...I didn't want to get tangled up in any incidents.
...For all I knew, Mion was just feeling too sick to get out of bed.
Maybe she was too ill to even answer the phone, and that was all Ooishi-san meant when he said she'd disappeared.
changeReason: Changes hypothetical self-reference ('If I never got out of bed') to description of Mion ('she was too ill'); different subject entirely.

TextENGNew: ...There's no point dwelling on things I can't do anything about.
``Rena, shall we get going?``
Rena and I started walking side by side.
On the way to school with Rena, everything I saw looked exactly the same as always, of course.
I felt like someone who'd been bedridden with illness, stepping outside for the first time in ages.
Without thinking, I found myself trying to take in every detail of my surroundings.
The more carefully I looked around, the more I realized that nothing had changed at all.
Maybe I really had just been overthinking everything until now...
As my confidence in the normalcy of it all grew, the tension in my shoulders and cheeks naturally began to melt away.
``Keiichi-kun, were you just thinking about something strange?``
``Wh-why would you say that?!@k@vS17/01/110100347. I wasn't thinking about anything like that!``
``Aah~, Keiichi-kun, you're blushing~.@k@vS17/02/110200156. How suspicious, how suspicious~.``
``I-I'm not blushing!@k@vS17/01/110100349. And there's nothing suspicious about it!``
``But you've had such a scary look on your face lately, and just now you were smiling.``
...Honestly, Rena, coming out with something like that out of nowhere.
But had I really been making those 'scary faces' she mentioned that much...?
Maybe she was right... It had been a long time since I'd felt this calm.
Still, that didn't give her any reason to call it something 'strange'... Heh, maybe it was time I teased her a little.
``Oh? So you noticed...``
``Ahahahaha, I knew it!@k@vS17/02/110200159. So then, so then... was it something weird after all?``
``I-it's not weird or anything...@k@vS17/01/110100352. I was just... looking at you and couldn't help but smile a little.``
``Wha...? W-w-w-why?! Why?@k@vS17/02/110200161. Why would looking at Rena make you smile? ...Smile? ...Smile?``
Rena's cheeks were already turning red.
``Lately... I've been kind of worn out...``
``Even though we see each other every day, I never had the peace of mind to actually look at your face properly.``
I could practically feel Rena's heart rate climbing by the second.
``So when I finally took a good look at you after all this time...@k@vS17/01/110100356. I thought, yeah, you really are cute.``
Rena turned a brilliant shade of red and looked ready to faint.
Mission accomplished...!@k Come to think of it, it had been quite a while since I'd teased Rena like that.
By the time we reached school, I was feeling not just normal, but lighter than I had in quite some time.
I flung open the classroom door with vigor.
Just then, I heard a faint 'snap' as if from somewhere far off.
The next moment...
*SPLAAASH*!!!
My entire upper body was drenched in some kind of liquid.
Normally, I would have been on guard before entering the classroom.
It seemed I really had let my defenses drop far more than usual.
There was a reason I said 'liquid' and not 'water'... Th-this smell... It r-reeeeeeks!!!
``S-Satokooo!!``
``...K-Keiichi-san, I never expected you to fall for it so easily!@k@vS17/04/110400054. I-it was supposed to be a little joke since you'd obviously dodge it!``
``How is this anywhere in the realm of a joke?!``
``...Dead stock milk is a terribly dangerous trap...@k@vS17/04/110400056. Surely you don't think I'd set one up seriously?!``
``My whole body is covered in your dead stock milk!!``
``I-I can't be held responsible for that!@k@vS17/04/110400058. It's your own fault for letting your guard down, Keiichi-san!!``
Despite blaming me, Satoko clearly sensed the danger she was in.
She was already trying to put distance between us...
But I had no intention of letting her escape.
Tap tap tap. Pitter-patter-patter.
Tap tap tap tap. Pitter-patter-patter-patter.
Tap tap tap, DASH!! Pitter-patter... Gotcha.
Too slow, Satoko.
*Thwack!*
``Wah, waaaaaaaaaaah!!!@k@vS17/04/110400060. Keiichi-san, aaah~!!``
``K-Keiichi-san gave me a forehead flick~~~!!``
``...Sheesh, this stuff seriously stinks~~!``
Rena, still reeling from the stench, was wisely keeping her distance from the spoiled milk's splash zone.
Even Rika-chan refused to come within a certain radius.
``...Poor, poor Keiichi.@k@vS17/05/110500069. ...But I don't want to come any closer.@k@vS17/05/110500070. Hurry and go change into your gym clothes. Mii~.``
changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: I made a beeline for the bathroom.
A forehead flick wasn't nearly enough payback for this suffering...!
Mark my words, Satoko, I'll have my revenge at today's club activity! U-ugh!
For the time being, I escaped into a bathroom stall.
Fortunately, there was nobody else in there.
If anyone saw me ducking into a stall covered head to toe in milk, I couldn't begin to imagine what rumors would start.
I pulled off my shirt and used it to wipe down my head, face, and upper body.
When I took off my pants, I discovered the milk had soaked all the way through to my underwear...
D-damn you, Satoko...
I had no choice but to strip off my underwear too, wiping the milk from my entire body with my uniform and boxers.
And the smell...@k It was definitely on the verge of going completely rotten...
And no matter how many times I wiped my hair, the stench clung stubbornly...
D-damn you, Satoko...
I pulled on the gym clothes I'd brought with me.
At this point, I couldn't afford to be picky about details.
After confirming no other students were around, I washed my hair at the sink.
Class had long since started.
But I couldn't afford to cut corners when dealing with this milk situation.
It was dead stock milk, after all.
D-damn you, Satoko...
I retreated back to the stall and let my imagination run wild as I scrubbed my hair dry with my hands...
I pictured Satoko, completely unable to move.
And of course, my own body was free to move however I pleased.
In one hand, I held a pot brimming with long-expired milk, radiating an ungodly stench.
All the power rested with me.
How would Satoko react in that scenario?!
The most satisfying scenario would be her putting up a spirited fight, as usual.
Satoko, unable to move an inch, yet refusing to abandon her defiant attitude...
Wearing an elegant smile, I tilted the pot toward Satoko's head...
And then, without a shred of mercy... poured it all over her...!!
In my fantasy, I exacted ample revenge upon Satoko.
That cheered me up a bit at least, Satoko!
...My hair was mostly dry now. Time to head back.
When I returned to the classroom, the lesson was already well into its second half.
When I explained I hadn't been feeling well, Chie-sensei forgave me without any fuss.
Spending that long in the bathroom wearing gym clothes did lend the excuse a certain credibility.
I cast a quick glance toward Mion's seat, but she still wasn't there.
After class, Rena told me that Mion had apparently called Chie-sensei to report she was staying home sick.
Rena seemed genuinely worried about Mion's health.
She didn't appear the least bit suspicious of the absence.
Maybe I really had just been overthinking everything lately.
During club activities, I scored my first major victory in a long time.
Naturally, my strategy from start to finish was laser-focused on taking Satoko down.@k Payback for the spoiled milk.
Fueled by that powerful motivation, I dominated completely. No one else stood a chance.
...I had so much fun.
...I laughed from the bottom of my heart.
...Time spent with my wonderful friends.
Wasn't this enough?
No -- this was the most precious thing in my life.
I would cherish every moment of it.
I would treasure my friends, and simply enjoy each day together...
